data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Closes 0.7% Lower at 5233.71
Hang Seng Index Closes 0.6% Lower at 24069.94
Synopsys Reports Lifting of US Export Restrictions Related to China; Shares Jump Premarket
data.description:
Synopsys said the Bureau of Industry and Security of the US Department of Commerce informed the company that export restrictions related to China have been "rescinded," effective immediately.

The company is working to restore access to the recently restricted products in China, it said late Wednesday.

"Synopsys is continuing to assess the impact of export restrictions related to China on its business, operating results, and financials," it added.

Shares of the company jumped more than 5% in premarket activity Thursday.

data.title: Datadog to Join S&P 500 July 9; Shares up Pre-Bell
data.description:
Datadog will join the S&P 500 July 9, S&P Global (SPGI) division S&P Dow Jones Indices said late Wednesday.

Datadog will replace Juniper Networks, which was recently acquired by Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE).

Datadog shares were up 12% in recent premarket activity Thursday.

data.title: NBTM New Materials' Unit Buys Land for 19 Million Yuan
data.description:
NBTM New Materials Group's unit, Shanxi Dongmu Magnetoelectric, acquired land in Linyi County, China for 19.1 million yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Thursday.

Shanxi Dongmu signed the land-use rights contract with the Linyi County Natural Resources Bureau.

The land will be the site for a factory that will produce 60,000 tons of soft magnetic materials every year. The construction is expected to start by July 30, 2026, and will be completed by July 30, 2029, the Chinese powder metallurgy new materials manufacturer said.

The company's shares closed over 4% higher.

data.title: Yang Ming Marine Transport Orders Containers to Expand Fleet
data.description:
Yang Ming Marine Transport ordered 40,400 containers from China International Marine Containers , and Guangdong Fuwa Equipment Manufacturing to expand its fleet.

The orders are worth between $112 million and $120 million in total, with unit prices ranging from $1,600 to $6,900 depending on the container type, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The purchases will be settled within 45 days of delivery and acceptance, it said.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 4 AM ET: Oil Futures Bounce in Defiance of Downbeat Data | U.S. ...
data.description:
Oil Futures Bounce in Defiance of Downbeat Data

Oil futures posted their sharpest gains in two weeks after Iran said it's suspending cooperation with the U.N. atomic agency, returning some of the risk premium that faded following last week's cease-fire.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Post Surprise Build as Exports Fall

U.S. crude oil inventories rose by 3.8 million barrels, as imports increased and exports fell sharply from the week before.

----

Can OPEC+ flood the world with crude? It's harder than oil traders think.

A portfolio manager pointed out a "unique" market development that could already be helping to tighten global crude inventories, even as major oil producers look to boost output for a fifth straight month.

----

Google Signs Deal to Buy Fusion Energy From Bill Gates-Backed Nuclear Startup

The tech giant is betting that Commonwealth Fusion Systems could start producing fusion power next decade.

----

Canada Loads First Ever LNG Cargo to Asia

LNG's role in Asia's energy mix looks set to keep growing as countries turn to the lower-carbon fuel to meet their green energy goals.

----

Wall Street Banks Nudge Oil Price Forecast Higher But Outlook Stays Bearish

Major Wall Street banks modestly lifted their oil-price forecasts after geopolitical risks flared in the Middle East, but concerns over a global supply glut and uncertain demand prospects continue to weigh on crude.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil futures, energy pricing regulation and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

Senate Megabill Stuns the Clean Energy Industry With New Tax on Wind and Solar

Elon Musk calls the Senate's latest version of the spending bill 'utterly insane and destructive.'

----

The Oil Tycoon and the Philosopher Threatening Big Oil's Bet on Carbon Capture

The fraught politics of carbon capture are dividing the oil industry.

----

Shell in Early Talks to Acquire Rival BP

A deal would be a landmark combination of two supermajor oil companies. Shell issued a formal stock exchange denial of takeover talks.

----

Trump Goes to Bat for Big Oil on Climate Rules in EU Trade Talks

The administration is targeting key EU greenhouse-gas regulations in trade negotiations with Europe.

----

Equinor and Partners to Invest Over $2 Billion in North Sea Oil and Gas Project

The energy major said the new subsea development at Farm Sor will mean more oil and gas can be supplied to Europe, bolstering energy security.

data.title: APAC Credit Conditions Sour as Tariffs Disrupt Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Uncertainty over tariffs and global trade have raised credit risks in APAC, Moody's Ratings says, as it shifts the region's sovereign creditworthiness outlook to negative from stable. Tariffs pose long-term credit risks for some APAC economies, harming their appeal and curbing foreign investment. Despite trade negotiations, Moody's has cut 2025 GDP growth views for 20 of 25 rated APAC economies, as well as 2026 views for many. Fiscal spending will likely rise to aid growth, slowing or halting fiscal consolidation. Weaker revenue--especially for trade-heavy countries--will further limit flexibility, while wider deficits will raise borrowing needs. Moody's would switch the outlook back to stable if trade talks sharply lower tariffs. Conversely, an escalation in tariffs, sharp rise in spreads, or protracted geopolitical conflicts would worsen conditions. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Weichai Power Cuts Buyback Price for Restricted Shares Under Incentive Scheme; Shares Up 1%
data.description:
Weichai Power slashed the buyback price for restricted shares under an incentive scheme to 5.252 yuan apiece from 5.599 yuan per share previously on Wednesday, a same-day Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Shares of the diesel engine manufacturer were up 1% in Thursday afternoon trading.

On the same day, the board approved the proposed repurchase and cancellation of all 820,000 granted but not unlocked restricted shares held by eight participants for over 4.3 million yuan plus accrued interest.

The approval is due to the participants undergoing re-designation, retirement or resignation.

data.title: Italian Shares Decline
data.description:
The FTSE MIB fell 0.4% to around 39,620 on Thursday, reversing some of the previous session’s gains and underperforming its European peers, weighed down by losses in financial stocks.

Investors remained focused on trade developments, supported by news that the US lifted certain chip design export restrictions on China—another sign of easing tensions and a possible trade truce.

On the bourse, declines were led by UniCredit and Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena, both down around 1%, followed by Intesa Sanpaolo (-0.5%), Bper Banca (-0.7%), and Banca Mediolanum (-0.4%).

These losses were partly offset by gains in the semiconductor sector, with STMicroelectronics advancing more than 1%.

On the data front, a fresh PMI survey showed that both the Italian private sector and services sector expanded at their weakest pace since March.

data.title: Wisdom Marine Lines' Pre-tax Net Income Dives 93% in June
data.description:
Wisdom Marine Lines posted a pre-tax net income of $1.3 million for June, plummeting 93% annually amid softer market conditions.

Revenue for the month fell 29% to $40.1 million, while operating income plunged 68% to $7.1 million, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Year-to-date, pre-tax profit nosedived 91% to $9.3 million, while revenue dropped 28% to $229.7 million, the company said.

The company cited dry-docking of seven vessels, contract renewals, and foreign exchange losses from yen and Taiwan dollar appreciation.

data.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares drop up to 2% on Macquarie downgrade
data.description:
Shares of Kotak Mahindra Bank fell over 2 percent in Thursday’s trade after global brokerage Macquarie downgraded the stock to ‘neutral’.

The scrip slipped as much as 2.1 percent to Rs 2,125.1 apiece on the NSE. The stock has declined 2.36 percent over the last two sessions.

Macquarie, in its latest note, downgraded Kotak Mahindra Bank to ‘neutral’ but raised the target price to Rs 2,300, according to CNBC-TV18.

The brokerage said margin concerns for banks are temporary and projected a 15 percent CAGR in earnings per share (EPS) over the next three years. It said it prefers large private lenders and sees selective opportunities in NBFCs.

Its top picks include HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Aditya Birla Capital, Power Finance Corporation, Shriram Housing Finance and LIC.

Sensex rises 400 pts, Nifty above 25,550: India-US trade deal optimism, other key factors behind market rise

Kotak Mahindra Bank was among the top laggards on the Nifty Bank index, which also traded in the red, even as broader headline indices remained positive.

Meanwhile, Macquarie upgraded PB Fintech to ‘neutral’ from ‘underperform’ with a revised target price of Rs 1,945 and downgraded IndusInd Bank to ‘underperform’ from ‘outperform’, cutting its target price to Rs 650.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Final PMI: French Private Sector Contraction Continues in June
data.description:
France's private sector contraction is "broadly unchanged" in June, as the service sector showed "modest" improvement in activity while the manufacturing sector saw a renewed downturn, final data from S&P Global and Hamburg Commercial Bank showed Thursday.

The HCOB France Composite PMI Output Index was 49.2, compared with the prior 49.3 and the flash estimate of 48.5.

Meanwhile, the services PMI rose to 49.6 from 48.9, against the initial reading of 48.7.

data.title: Theratechnologies to Be Acquired by Future Pak Affiliate for Up to $254 Million
data.description:
Theratechnologies said late Wednesday it agreed to be acquired by CB Biotechnology, an affiliate of Future Pak, in a transaction valued at up to $254 million.

Under the terms, the company said shareholders will receive $3.01 per share in cash, a 126% premium over the share price before the initial proposal, along with a contingent value right that could pay up to an additional $1.19 per share if specific financial milestones are met over the next three years.

The company said the deal comes from a formal sale process led by a special committee of independent directors, adding that the contingent value right will not be tradable or listed and does not offer ownership or voting rights.

The acquisition, which is funded by a mix of Future Pak's available cash and a $220 million debt facility, will proceed through a court-approved plan of arrangement and is expected to close in fiscal Q4, the company said.

Following the transaction, subject to shareholder and court approval, Theratechnologies said it will become a private company, cease being a public issuer in Canada and the US, and its shares will no longer be traded on the TSX or Nasdaq.

If the company accepts a better offer, it would owe a $6 million termination fee, while a $12 million reverse breakup fee would apply if the buyer fails to close under specific conditions, it added.

data.title: Eurozone Services Activity Revised to Show Expansion
data.description:
The HCOB Eurozone Services PMI rose to 50.5 in June of 2025 from 49.7 in the previous month, revised higher from the flash estimate of 50 and firmly above the initial market expectations of 50 to reflect a slight expansion in the area's services activity.

Output was higher in the period as companies depleted their outstanding work, offsetting the further decline in new business captures in the period.

Still, companies continued increasing their employment levels to extend the sequence to over four years.

On the price front, input costs rose at a softer place for the third time in four months, although they remained at elevated historical levels, driving companies to raise output charges the most in three months.

Looking ahead, business expectations continued to recover from April's low, when the threat of tariffs by the US jeopardized a large portion of Eurozone export clients.

data.title: ZAWYA: Abu Dhabi stock exchange to list MENA’s first digital bond
data.description:
Staff Writer

The Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange (ADX), the second-largest exchange in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), will list the first-ever distributed ledger technology (DLT)-based bond in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. 

The bond is issued by First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB) using HSBC Orion, a digital assets platform and will be listed on ADX.

HSBC Orion is operated by the Central Moneymarkets Unit (CMU) in Hong Kong, which is supported by top international law firms, ADX said in a statement.

Global investors can access the digital bond through accounts held with CMU, Euroclear and Clearstream, onboarding onto HSBC Orion or via their existing custodian who can participate through one of the above options.

Digital bonds, fixed-income securities issued and recorded on the blockchain, offer operational efficiencies, improved settlement cycles, reduced counterparty risk, enhanced security, and increased transparency for institutional investors.

HSBC acted as the sole global coordinator, lead manager and bookrunner on the transaction.

Abdulla Salem Alnuaimi, Group Chief Executive Officer of ADX, said the initiative not only expands access to institutional-grade digital instruments, but lays the foundation for a broader class of tokenised assets, including green bonds, sukuk, and real estate-linked products. 

(Editing by Seban Scaria seban.scaria@lseg.com )

Disclaimer: This article is provided for informational purposes only. The content does not provide tax, legal or investment advice or opinion regarding the suitability, value or profitability of any particular security, portfolio or investment strategy. Read our full disclaimer policy here.

data.title: Libya's AGOCO completes Hamada-Zawiya oil pipeline repairs
data.description:
Libya's Arabian Gulf Oil Company (AGOCO) has completed repairs to a crude oil pipeline leak along the 18-inch Hamada-Zawiya line, it said on Thursday, after an oil leak in late May.

The leak prompted full isolation of the pipeline to halt the flow of crude, followed by the suction of oil from the pipeline and its return to the Tahara field for repumping.

Zawiya, 40 km (25 miles) west of Tripoli, is home to Libya's biggest functioning refinery, with capacity of 120,000 barrels per day (bpd).

The refinery is connected to the country's 300,000 bpd Sharara oilfield.

data.title: Tecom Group Halts Dubai Bourse Trading due to Board Meeting
data.description:
Tecom Group (DFM:TECOM) suspended trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 10:30 am local time on Thursday due to a board meeting.

The business district developer will resume trading once the meeting results are published, according to a same-day release.
